description: Fix documentation inaccuracies, missing features, and incorrect technical details found during a comprehensive audit of the docs site against the current codebase.
qualityChecks: cd docs && npm run build && npm run type-check && npm run lint && npm run knip && npm run format:check

userStories.title: Add 2 missing browser tools and fix tool counts in browser-tools.mdx
userStories.description: As a user reading the browser tools reference, I want to see all 39 browser tools documented so that I know about browser_get_websocket_frames and browser_export_har.
acceptanceCriteria: browser-tools.mdx title/description references 39 browser tools, not 37

A new row for `browser_get_websocket_frames` is added to the Network Capture table with description: 'Get WebSocket frames captured since browser_enable_network_capture was called. Each frame includes URL, direction, data, opcode, and timestamp.'

A new row for `browser_export_har` is added to the Network Capture table with description: 'Export captured network traffic as a HAR 1.2 JSON file. Optionally includes WebSocket frames. The resulting JSON can be opened in Chrome DevTools or any HAR-compatible tool.'
The Network Capture count in the Summary table is updated from 3 to 5
The Total count in the Summary table is updated from 37 to 39
cd docs && npm run build passes
cd docs && npm run type-check passes
cd docs && npm run lint passes
cd docs && npm run knip passes
cd docs && npm run format:check passes
userStories.notes:
File: docs/content/docs/reference/browser-tools.mdx

The two missing tools exist in the source at:
- platform/mcp-server/src/browser-tools/get-websocket-frames.ts — tool name: browser_get_websocket_frames. Params: tabId (number), clear (boolean, optional, default false). Description: 'Get WebSocket frames captured since browser_enable_network_capture was called on this tab.'
- platform/mcp-server/src/browser-tools/export-har.ts — tool name: browser_export_har. Params: tabId (number), clear (boolean, optional, default false), includeWebSocketFrames (boolean, optional, default false). Description: 'Export captured network traffic as a HAR 1.2 JSON file.'

Both belong in the 'Network Capture' section (lines 164-189). Add rows after browser_get_network_requests.

Also fix:
- Line 3 (frontmatter description): change '37' to '39'
- Summary table at the end: Network Capture count 3 → 5, Total 37 → 39

userStories.title: Fix extension script timeout in architecture.mdx
userStories.description: As a platform contributor reading the architecture docs, I want the timeout table to show the correct default script execution timeout (25s, not 295s) so I understand the actual system behavior.
acceptanceCriteria: The timeouts table in architecture.mdx shows 'Extension script execution' with default 25 seconds and maximum 295 seconds (matching SCRIPT_TIMEOUT_MS=25000 and MAX_SCRIPT_TIMEOUT_MS=295000 from the source)
cd docs && npm run build passes
cd docs && npm run type-check passes
cd docs && npm run lint passes
cd docs && npm run knip passes
cd docs && npm run format:check passes
userStories.notes:
File: docs/content/docs/contributing/architecture.mdx — around line 364 in the timeouts table.

The table currently shows 'Extension script execution' with default '295 seconds'. The actual values from platform/browser-extension/src/constants.ts are:
- SCRIPT_TIMEOUT_MS = 25_000 (25 seconds) — the default timeout
- MAX_SCRIPT_TIMEOUT_MS = 295_000 (295 seconds) — the absolute max when progress resets the timer

The table should show the same two-column pattern used for 'Tool dispatch' (which shows 30s default and 5min max). Change the row to show default 25 seconds and maximum 295 seconds. Follow the pattern of the Tool dispatch row which already shows both default and maximum.

userStories.title: Fix concurrent dispatch behavior in mcp-server.mdx
userStories.description: As a plugin developer reading the MCP server reference, I want to know that excess concurrent tool calls are rejected (not queued) so I can design my tools accordingly.
acceptanceCriteria: mcp-server.mdx correctly states that excess concurrent tool calls per plugin are rejected with an error, not queued
cd docs && npm run build passes
cd docs && npm run type-check passes
cd docs && npm run lint passes
cd docs && npm run knip passes
cd docs && npm run format:check passes
userStories.notes:
File: docs/content/docs/reference/mcp-server.mdx — search for text about concurrent dispatches per plugin.

The docs currently say something like 'Up to 5 concurrent tool calls per plugin; additional calls are queued.' The actual behavior in platform/mcp-server/src/mcp-tool-dispatch.ts (lines 364-375) REJECTS excess calls with an error response: 'Too many concurrent dispatches for plugin ... Wait for in-flight requests to complete.' It does NOT queue them.

Change 'queued' to 'rejected with an error' (or similar phrasing that accurately describes the behavior). The agent should return a message like 'Wait for in-flight requests to complete' so callers know to retry.

userStories.title: Fix /ws-info response and health response examples in mcp-server.mdx
userStories.description: As a contributor reading the MCP server reference, I want the endpoint response examples to include all fields that the server actually returns so the docs match the implementation.
acceptanceCriteria: The /ws-info endpoint documentation mentions the conditional wsSecret field in the response
The /health endpoint's authenticated response example includes browserToolCount, pluginToolCount, and pluginDetails[].tools fields
cd docs && npm run build passes
cd docs && npm run type-check passes
cd docs && npm run lint passes
cd docs && npm run knip passes
cd docs && npm run format:check passes
userStories.notes:
File: docs/content/docs/reference/mcp-server.mdx

1. /ws-info response (around line 218-221): Currently shows only `{ "wsUrl": "ws://localhost:9515/ws" }`. The actual handler (platform/mcp-server/src/http-routes.ts line 243) also conditionally includes `wsSecret` when a secret is configured. Add a note explaining that the response includes `wsSecret` when authentication is configured.

2. /health authenticated response example (around lines 148-185): The example is missing several fields from the actual response (platform/mcp-server/src/http-routes.ts lines 291-319):
  - `browserToolCount` (number)
  - `pluginToolCount` (number)
  - `pluginDetails[].tools` (string[] of prefixed tool names)
  Add these to the example JSON and/or the field descriptions table. Don't add every internal field — focus on the ones useful to users and contributors.

userStories.title: Add plugins.changed notification to WebSocket protocol tables
userStories.description: As a contributor reading the WebSocket protocol documentation, I want to see the plugins.changed notification so the protocol reference is complete.
acceptanceCriteria: mcp-server.mdx WebSocket protocol table (Server → Extension section) includes a row for `plugins.changed` notification
If architecture.mdx has a similar protocol table, it also includes `plugins.changed`
cd docs && npm run build passes
cd docs && npm run type-check passes
cd docs && npm run lint passes
cd docs && npm run knip passes
cd docs && npm run format:check passes
userStories.notes:
Two files to check:
1. docs/content/docs/reference/mcp-server.mdx — search for the WebSocket protocol table (Server → Extension direction). Add a row for `plugins.changed` — it's a notification sent from server to extension when the plugin registry changes (e.g., after POST /reload). The extension uses it to update its plugin list and re-inject adapters.
2. docs/content/docs/contributing/architecture.mdx — check if it has a similar wire protocol table that should also include plugins.changed.

The source reference for this notification is in platform/mcp-server/src/extension-handlers.ts (sendPluginUpdate function) which sends a JSON-RPC notification with method 'plugins.changed'.

userStories.title: Fix Claude Code MCP config path in mcp-server.mdx
userStories.description: As a user configuring their MCP client, I want consistent config file paths across all docs pages so I don't get confused.
acceptanceCriteria: mcp-server.mdx uses the same Claude Code MCP config path as quick-start.mdx and install/index.mdx (which is ~/.claude/settings/mcp.json)
cd docs && npm run build passes
cd docs && npm run type-check passes
cd docs && npm run lint passes
cd docs && npm run knip passes
cd docs && npm run format:check passes
userStories.notes:
File: docs/content/docs/reference/mcp-server.mdx — around line 533.

The page mentions adding MCP server config to `~/.claude.json`. Other docs pages (quick-start.mdx around line 51, install/index.mdx around line 43) use `~/.claude/settings/mcp.json` which is the correct/newer location for Claude Code MCP server configuration.

Update the reference in mcp-server.mdx to use `~/.claude/settings/mcp.json` for consistency. Search for all occurrences of `~/.claude.json` in the file and replace with the correct path.
